luke.story: Luke shares why access to hands-on recovery care matters after service.

adam.quote: “A major part of my success was the treatments and therapy I received from Dr. Vas.”
adam.attribution: Adam Folker · Collegiate & Professional Basketball Player
adam.story: During my sophomore year in college, I had hip surgery that was potentially season-ending and even career-ending. My coaches referred me to Dr. Vas, who was able to take care of me and eventually get me back to playing within a short few weeks. That season I ended up winning hustle player of the year award. Throughout my career, I saw Dr. Vas on various occasions for injuries that came up, along with maintenance and preventive care. I was able to play competitively at the top level.

leadership.name: Dr. Vasili “Dr. Vas” Gatsinaris, D.C.
paragraphs: Dr. Vas earned his Doctorate of Chiropractic from the Los Angeles College of Chiropractic in 2001 and has more than 25 years of experience helping people recover and rebuild.

Over his career, he has worked with everyone from professional athletes to first responders, developing a hands-on, integrative approach focused on restoring mobility and getting people back to life stronger and faster.

He is co-owner of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center in Newport Beach, a full-service wellness facility offering chiropractic, physical and massage therapy, and nutrition — the same integrative recovery care 22 Strong helps fund.

leadership.name: Travis Mayfield
paragraphs: A Purple Heart Army veteran who served with the 101st Airborne, Travis is Co-Owner and President of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center. He helped bring 22 Strong together and leads its mission to make recovery care accessible to those who served and to others working to rebuild.
His own path from service informs how 22 Strong shows up for the people it serves: with respect, follow-through, and care that doesn’t stop at the first appointment.
